items.title: Seventeen’s V8 embraces challenge, growth on debut EP ‘V8’
items.snippet: Seventeen’s new unit V8 — consisting of The8 and Vernon — embarks on a new musical journey with the release of its self-titled EP on Monday. According to Pledis Entertainment, the...
items.description: Seventeen’s new unit V8 — consisting of The8 and Vernon — embarks on a new musical journey with the release of its self-titled EP on Monday. According to Pledis Entertainment, the album follows a theme of youth shaped by uncertainty, weaving together moments of wandering, confusion, recovery and growth. items.title: Biggest-ever Seoul concerts just the beginning for TWS
items.snippet: Boy group TWS took another major step in its rise as one of K-pop’s leading fifth-generation boy groups, holding its first solo concert at Kspo Dome just over two years after its d...
items.description: Boy group TWS took another major step in its rise as one of K-pop’s leading fifth-generation boy groups, holding its first solo concert at Kspo Dome just over two years after its debut. The six-member group held its “24/7:For:You” concert on Saturday and Sunday at Kspo Dome in Songpa-gu, southern Seoul, drawing a combined audience of 18,000 across two sold-out shows. items.title: BoyNextDoor hits career-best with 1st LP
items.snippet: BoyNextDoor has wrapped up promotions for the group's first studio album, “Home,” as of Sunday. The LP was released on June 8, preceded by prerelease “Ddok Ddok Ddok,” and debuted...
items.description: BoyNextDoor has wrapped up promotions for the group's first studio album, “Home,” as of Sunday. The LP was released on June 8, preceded by prerelease “Ddok Ddok Ddok,” and debuted on Billboard 200 at the group's highest-ever No. 16. The eight-track set landed atop Oricon’s Daily, Weekly and Weekly Combined Album Rankings as well as a series of major music charts at home and abroad. The LP sold over 1 million copies in the first week, becoming the six-piece act’s fourth consecutive million-seller

items.title: Cortis garners 200m Spotify streams with ‘Go!’
items.snippet: Big Hit Music on Sunday announced that Cortis hit 200 million streams on Spotify with “Go!” This is the first song from the rookie boy band to achieve the feat. “Go!” is a B-side t...
items.description: Big Hit Music on Sunday announced that Cortis hit 200 million streams on Spotify with “Go!” This is the first song from the rookie boy band to achieve the feat. “Go!” is a B-side track from the group's debut EP “Color Outside the Lines,” which rolled out in September last year. It was dropped ahead of the full album release, debuting on Melon’s Top 100 at No. 98 and on its Daily Songs Chart at No. 95. It was one of the best K-pop songs of the year selected by Billboard magazine and Spotify. The

items.title: Le Sserafim, Illit, Katseye collaboration finds global success beyond fandoms
items.snippet: “Iconic by Mistake,” a collaboration between Le Sserafim, Illit and Katseye, continues to build momentum on global charts, becoming one of the biggest K-pop girl group releases of...
items.description: “Iconic by Mistake,” a collaboration between Le Sserafim, Illit and Katseye, continues to build momentum on global charts, becoming one of the biggest K-pop girl group releases of the year while demonstrating the commercial potential of cross-label collaborations. The digital single climbed four spots to No. 21 on Spotify’s global weekly songs chart for June 19-25 after debuting at No. 25 the previous week. It also ranked No. 32 on Spotify’s weekly chart for the US, reflecting sustained momentum

items.title: Big Bang’s August gig sells out in minutes
items.snippet: Big Bang's three-date concert in August sold out in minutes when tickets went on sale Thursday, according to label YG Entertainment. The concert in Goyang, Gyeonggi Province, will...
items.description: Big Bang's three-date concert in August sold out in minutes when tickets went on sale Thursday, according to label YG Entertainment. The concert in Goyang, Gyeonggi Province, will mark the beginning of the group’s world tour and is a celebration of its 20th anniversary. The ticket sales drew approximately 210,000 concurrent users. The tour will run until February 2027, and the trio — G Dragon, Taeyang and Daesung — will zip around 18 cities across the world with 31 shows scheduled. The group is

items.title: And2ble signs with Sony Music ahead of Japan debut
items.snippet: And2ble has signed with Epic Records Japan, marking its entry into the Japanese market. YH Entertainment announced Friday that And2ble had signed a contract with Epic Records Japan...
items.description: And2ble has signed with Epic Records Japan, marking its entry into the Japanese market. YH Entertainment announced Friday that And2ble had signed a contract with Epic Records Japan, a record label under Sony Music Labels, a division of Sony Music Entertainment Japan. And2ble, a five-member boy group, debuted in May with the mini album "Sequence 01: Curiosity." The album surpassed 730,000 sales in its first week on Hanteo Chart, Korea's real-time album sales chart, ranking fourth among debut albu

items.title: I-dle gives taste of all tracks from 9th EP
items.snippet: Girl group I-dle uploaded a snippet of its ninth EP, “We Made,” via agency Cube Entertainment on Friday. The preview includes parts of all six songs from the forthcoming EP, from t...
items.description: Girl group I-dle uploaded a snippet of its ninth EP, “We Made,” via agency Cube Entertainment on Friday. The preview includes parts of all six songs from the forthcoming EP, from the main track “Gimme Dat Love” to B-side tracks “Morning” and “Love is Pain” and prereleases “Mono (feat. skaiwater)” and “Crow.” The mini album is set to be fully unveiled on July 6, fronted by what the group is promoting as a summer song. items.title: G.Na returns to music 10 years after sex-for-money scandal
items.snippet: Korean Canadian singer and actress G.Na is set to return to music with her first official release since 2016, nearly a decade after a prostitution scandal brought her career to a h...
items.description: Korean Canadian singer and actress G.Na is set to return to music with her first official release since 2016, nearly a decade after a prostitution scandal brought her career to a halt. The singer revealed Tuesday through a social media post that the song she will remake for her comeback, her 2010 debut hit "I'll Back Off So You Can Live Better," was selected by fans. items.title: Cortis joins F1 Singapore opening lineup as sole K-pop group
items.snippet: Cortis will perform on the opening night of the Formula 1 Singapore Grand Prix race weekend in October. The F1 Singapore GP unveiled an updated concert line-up on Thursday for its...
items.description: Cortis will perform on the opening night of the Formula 1 Singapore Grand Prix race weekend in October. The F1 Singapore GP unveiled an updated concert line-up on Thursday for its race weekend at Marina Bay this fall. Cortis is the only K-pop group named in the announcement. Other newly listed performers include Janet Jackson, James Arthur, Zara Larsson and SNSD's Hyoyeon. Cortis will kick off the F1 weekend event on October 9 along with JJ Lin, Spacey Jane, Borderline, Shingo Nakamura, Split En

items.title: From fan to co-star: Chase Infiniti stars in Ateez’s ‘Bad’
items.snippet: Ateez has released two music video teasers for the main track of its upcoming album "Golden Hour: Part 5" featuring actor Chase Infinity. The first teaser, released on Wednesday, o...
items.description: Ateez has released two music video teasers for the main track of its upcoming album "Golden Hour: Part 5" featuring actor Chase Infinity. The first teaser, released on Wednesday, opens at Infinity's wedding. However, eight rings representing the eight members of Ateez are already on her ring finger. As the groom tries to add another ring, one of the members interrupts the ceremony. The second teaser, out Thursday, shows more chemistry between Infiniti and Ateez members. items.title: Kim Gun-mo returns with first new single in 10 years
items.snippet: Legendary singer-songwriter Kim Gun-mo will release a new digital single, "How Far Are You?," on July 1, his first new song in a decade, his agency Guneum Company announced Thursda...
items.description: Legendary singer-songwriter Kim Gun-mo will release a new digital single, "How Far Are You?," on July 1, his first new song in a decade, his agency Guneum Company announced Thursday. The release is Kim's first since his 2016 EP "50," which commemorated the 25th anniversary of his debut. "How Far Are You?" is a remake of the title track from Korean folk singer Jeon Young's 1977 debut album.
